blocks.content:
In this topic, you will practice everything you learned in this lesson.

You already know how to use:

`if`
`else`
`else if`

Now you will use them in small tasks.

The tasks go from simple to more complex.

Create a variable `temperature` and set it to 10.

If the temperature is greater than 0, print:

"Above zero"

Use only `if`.

Create a variable `age` and set it to 16.

If the age is 18 or more, print:

"Access allowed"

Otherwise print:

"Access denied"

Use `if` and `else`.

Create a variable `score` and set it to 65.

If `score` is greater than or equal to 80, print:

"Excellent"

Otherwise, if `score` is greater than or equal to 50, print:

"Pass"

Otherwise print:

"Fail"

Use `if`, `else if`, and `else`.

Create a variable `age` and set it to 17.

If the age is less than 18, print:

"Too young"

Otherwise, if the age is equal to 18, print:

"Just allowed"

Otherwise print:

"Access allowed"

Common mistake:

Using multiple `if` statements instead of `else if`.

The program can still run, but it may work incorrectly.

If you use several `if` statements, the program can run more than one block.

Use `else if` when you want to choose only one correct option.
